message:
refactor(core): Migrate tokio::process::Command to runtime_compat abstraction

Replaces direct tokio::process::Command imports with the runtime_compat
process module across three source files (cass.rs, caut.rs, wezterm.rs),
completing the async runtime abstraction layer for process spawning.

This ensures all process invocations go through the runtime_compat
abstraction, enabling flexible runtime swapping and improving testability
of process-spawning code paths without hard tokio coupling.

Also updates runtime_compat_surface_guard.rs to:
- Allow tokio::process:: references in the confinement test whitelist
- Assert that runtime_compat.rs exports a pub mod process, enforcing
  the abstraction contract at the test level

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -99,6 +99,7 @@ fn tokio_async_runtime_primitives_stay_confined_to_runtime_compat_module() {
         &allowed,
         &[
             "tokio::select!",
+            "tokio::process::",
             "tokio::signal::",
             "tokio::time::sleep",
             "tokio::time::timeout",
@@ -159,6 +160,10 @@ fn web_and_cli_async_surfaces_route_through_runtime_compat() {
         runtime_compat.contains("pub mod signal"),
         "runtime_compat.rs must continue to expose a signal bridge while this migration contract is active"
     );
+    assert!(
+        runtime_compat.contains("pub mod process"),
+        "runtime_compat.rs must continue to expose a process bridge while this migration contract is active"
+    );
     assert!(
         web.contains("use crate::runtime_compat::{mpsc, select, signal, sleep, task, timeout};"),
         "web.rs must import runtime_compat bridges for async runtime operations"
